content: read reviews/130.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/130.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/130.md.

    19→- [x] Bibliography entries: `[Er97b]` and `[AnEr45]` with full citation details.
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos130 ... end Erdos130`.

    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `Concyclic` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    30→- [x] `ErdosGeneralPosition` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    31→- [x] `intDistGraph` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    32→- [x] `intDistGraph` is marked `noncomputable` (involves real-valued distance computations).
    33→- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
    34→- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `Concyclic`, `ErdosGeneralPosition`.
    35→- [x] Function returning a type uses `lowerCamelCase`: `intDistGraph`.
    36→- N/A: No local notation needed.
    37→- N/A: No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).
    38→
    39→## 6. Theorem Statements
    40→
    41→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    42→- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_130` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    43→- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
    44→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 51]`.
    45→- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
    46→- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er97b]` and `[AnEr45]`.
    47→
    48→### `answer()` usage
    49→
    50→The problem asks: *"Can the chromatic number of G be infinite?"* — this is a yes/no question (**Case 1**).
    51→
    52→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked.
    53→- [x] `answer(sorry)` is used correctly (problem is open/unsolved).
    54→- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (`∃ A, A.Infinite ∧ ErdosGeneralPosition A ∧ chromaticNumber = ⊤`), so `answer(True)` would mean "yes, the chromatic number can be infinite."
    55→- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(sorry)` (the `∃` is on the RHS of `↔`, not as theorem parameters).
    56→
    57→## 7. Category Attribute
    58→
    59→- [x] Has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
    60→- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
    61→- [x] Research open theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.
    62→
    63→## 8. AMS Attribute
    64→
    65→- [x] Has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 51`.
    66→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 51).
    67→- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 51.
    68→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    69→- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 51 (geometry) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.
    70→
    71→## 9. Proof Bodies
    72→
    73→- [x] Open conjecture uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
    74→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    75→- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
    76→- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. The docstring's note about the Anning–Erdős theorem provides mathematical context, not a proof sketch.
    77→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    78→
    79→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    80→
    81→- N/A: No variants present. Only one main theorem.
    82→
    83→## 11. Code Quality
    84→
    85→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    86→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `∧`, `¬`, `≠`, `↔`, `→`.
    87→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    88→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    89→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    90→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    91→
    92→## 12. Build Verification
    93→
    94→- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
    95→- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.
    96→
    97→## Summary
    98→
    99→**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)
   100→
   101→The file is clean and well-structured. All stylistic, structural, and semantic checklist items pass. The `answer()` usage correctly models the yes/no question. Custom definitions (`Concyclic`, `ErdosGeneralPosition`, `intDistGraph`) are well-documented, appropriately named, and `noncomputable` where needed. The `intDistGraph` definition correctly encodes the symmetry and irreflexivity proofs inline. AMS codes 5 (combinatorics) and 51 (geometry) are appropriate for this problem.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/130.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 130`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/130](https://www.erdosproblems.com/130)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (infinite point set in general position, integer-distance graph, chromatic number question).
- [x] Bibliography entries: `[Er97b]` and `[AnEr45]` with full citation details.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos130 ... end Erdos130`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos130`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `Concyclic` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `ErdosGeneralPosition` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `intDistGraph` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] `intDistGraph` is marked `noncomputable` (involves real-valued distance computations).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types/Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `Concyclic`, `ErdosGeneralPosition`.
- [x] Function returning a type uses `lowerCamelCase`: `intDistGraph`.
- N/A: No local notation needed.
- N/A: No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks (optional).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_130` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` convention.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 51]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er97b]` and `[AnEr45]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem asks: *"Can the chromatic number of G be infinite?"* — this is a yes/no question (**Case 1**).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ↔ P`, where `P` formalizes the question as asked.
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is used correctly (problem is open/unsolved).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (`∃ A, A.Infinite ∧ ErdosGeneralPosition A ∧ chromaticNumber = ⊤`), so `answer(True)` would mean "yes, the chromatic number can be infinite."
- [x] Quantifiers appear **after** `answer(sorry)` (the `∃` is on the RHS of `↔`, not as theorem parameters).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research open`.
- [x] Research open theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5 51`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5, 51).
- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 51.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 51 (geometry) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- N/A: File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems. The docstring's note about the Anning–Erdős theorem provides mathematical context, not a proof sketch.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants present. Only one main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℝ`, `ℕ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `∧`, `¬`, `≠`, `↔`, `→`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] Custom linters — not verified in this review.

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. All stylistic, structural, and semantic checklist items pass. The `answer()` usage correctly models the yes/no question. Custom definitions (`Concyclic`, `ErdosGeneralPosition`, `intDistGraph`) are well-documented, appropriately named, and `noncomputable` where needed. The `intDistGraph` definition correctly encodes the symmetry and irreflexivity proofs inline. AMS codes 5 (combinatorics) and 51 (geometry) are appropriate for this problem.

content.text: The review passed — no changes needed.
